subject: LU-15815 llite: disable fast_read and workaround

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Seems reasonable.

It might be possible to selectively disable fast reads on mmap files, if this is the only bad combination, or find a better long-term solution that allows them to work together, but that can be decided  after this working correctly.

Patch Set 1:

it looks like sanity 101j and 271a are 100% failures with this patch

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

> It might be possible to selectively disable fast reads on mmap files, if this is the only bad combination, or find a better long-term solution that allows them to work together, but that can be decided  after this working correctly.

Bad news and bad news.

1. Even with fast_read disabled we can still reproduce LU-15815. It's the workaround from LU-14541 that causes the SIGBUS.

2. The change from LU-14541 is protecting us from read/write sequential consistency issues that happen even with fast read disabled. I have a reliable reproducer for this too.

Patch Set 1:

I can confirm that fast reads are unrelated and the issue reproduces with fast reads disabled. We had similar reports ( LU-15819 ).

Patch Set 1:

> I think that means we must clear uptodate, because the page can still be found but is not safe to access

Clearing page uptodate bit violates the mm rules. In the original ticket, it was mentioned that it is possible to see random EIOs with ClearPageUptodate(). Unfortunately, no easy alternative solution was suggested at that time.
Removed Code-Review-1 by John L. Hammond <jhammond@whamcloud.com>

Patch Set 1:

Removing my -1 since this it probably the less worse thing to do right now.
Uploaded patch set 2.

parents.subject: LU-15761 obdclass: fix locking in llog_cat_refresh()

commit.subject: LU-15815 llite: disable fast_read and workaround
commit.message:
LU-15815 llite: disable fast_read and workaround

Revert the fast_read stale data workaround from LU-14541 and disable
fast_read by default. The workaround causes applications to receive
spurious SIGBUGs when reclaim is concurrent with mmap page fault
handlers. We disable fast read to avoid the stale data issue entirely.

This reverts commit f2a16793fa4316fc9ccdc46bcfe54f6b8d1e442b and
re-exposes us to the consistency issues described in LU-14541.

Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <jhammond@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I716b1fd6ab22242b9267b8883f0371a360aaecef
